Document:
# Spell Fallback System
The Spell Fallback System handles what happens when a spell grants a stat modifier that pushes the character's stat past its maximum.
## The Problem
If a character has INT 9 and a spell grants +2 INT, the effective stat would be 11 — but the cap is 10. The +1 overflow needs to go somewhere.
## The Solution
When a modifier causes overflow, the system: 1. Caps the effective stat at MaxStat (10) 2. Calculates the overflow amount 3. Adds the overflow as AvailablePoints the player can spend
// If adding a modifier would exceed MaxStat: int projected = (BaseStat + currentModifiers + newModifier); if (projected > MaxStat) { int overflow = projected - MaxStat; component.AvailablePoints += overflow; }
## Spell Tier Interaction
Each spell tier in a skill tree can add stat modifiers. When stacking multiple tiers, overflow accumulates incrementally.
[bold]Example:[/bold] - Elf base INT: 8 - T1 spell: +1 INT (effective: 9) - T2 spell: +1 INT (effective: 10, capped) - T3 spell: +1 INT (effective: 10, overflow: +1 available point)
## Content Under Construction
Full spell fallback documentation coming soon. Refer to the modifier source code: [mono]Content.Shared/_OE14/CharacterStats/OE14CharacterStatsSystem.cs[/mono]
